commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `runReplyAgent` to emit fallback notices for source-preserving subagent/inter-session runs without mutating sticky fallback state, and updates the focused E2E test coverage.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +49. Total +55 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main gates fallback notices behind non-preserved user-facing session state, and `subagent_announce` is preserved inter-session provenance. A live preserved subagent fallback run has not been proven in this review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review remains because the blockers are contributor real behavior proof and fallback-notice visibility policy, not a narrow code repair automation can supply.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): surface preserved fallback notices
target.body:
## Summary

- Decouple model fallback notice emission from user-facing fallback-state persistence for source-preserving inter-session/subagent handoffs.
- Preserve-mode runs now emit the current run's fallback notice when the run records fallback attempts, even if the user-facing session already has the same sticky fallback pair.
- Keep sticky fallback state writes and fallback-cleared notices gated to non-preserving user-facing runs.

Refs #94919.

## Root Cause

`runReplyAgent` used `fallbackTransition.fallbackTransitioned` for both persisted fallback-state changes and visible fallback notices. That transition flag is derived from the user-facing session's stored `fallbackNotice*` fields. For source-preserving provenance such as `subagent_announce`, OpenClaw intentionally does not mutate those fields, so fallback notices could be suppressed or buried when the source run fell back.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- GitHub `Real behavior proof` workflow passed on exact PR head `39d5ee55683689b7f45083e865be3c68a9e72aa7`: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27857001621
- One Codex autoreview pass found a preserve-mode sticky-state suppression gap; this patch includes the follow-up fix before opening the draft.

## Proof status

Structured proof parsing is passing on the current PR head. ClawSweeper still requests stronger real behavior proof before merge: a redacted terminal/log/screenshot/recording showing a real preserved subagent or async fallback run where the fallback notice reaches the originating visible reply.

That Telegram/Desktop-style artifact is **not included yet**. This PR keeps the local CLI smoke and focused regression coverage below, but it does not claim those are sufficient to satisfy the preserved-run real-proof ask. The Mantis workflow documented for this is `Mantis Telegram Desktop Proof`, triggered by the `mantis: telegram-visible-proof` label plus a comment such as:

```text
@openclaw-mantis telegram desktop proof
```

This contributor account cannot add the required Mantis label; GitHub returned `403` for `AddLabelsToLabelable`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Source-preserving inter-session/subagent fallback runs should return the `Model Fallback` notice for the current fallback run without mutating the user-facing session's fallback/model override state.

Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw source checkout at PR head `39d5ee55683689b7f45083e865be3c68a9e72aa7`, with repository dependencies already installed. I ran the actual OpenClaw CLI entrypoint after the patch earlier in this PR, and reran the focused source-preserving fallback regression plus lint on the current head. I did not have a live provider/channel setup that could produce a real preserved subagent or async fallback run.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
pnpm openclaw --help
pnpm openclaw agent --help
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix: Copied live terminal output from the patched OpenClaw CLI and current focused checks:

```text
$ pnpm openclaw --help
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs --help

OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (8a8a3a8) — All your chats, one OpenClaw.

Usage: openclaw [options] [command]

Commands:
  agent                Run one agent turn via the Gateway
  status               Show Gateway, channel, model, and recent-session status
  plugins *            Install, enable, disable, and inspect plugins

$ pnpm openclaw agent --help
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --help

OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (8a8a3a8) — All your chats, one OpenClaw.

Usage: openclaw agent [options]

Run an agent turn via the Gateway (use --local for embedded)

$ node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts
# no output; exit 0

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  61 passed (61)

$ git diff --check
# no output; exit 0
```

Supplemental behavior-specific regression coverage on the same PR head drives the source-preserving fallback path for `subagent_announce` with no stored fallback state and with a preexisting matching stored fallback pair, then asserts `Model Fallback` payload delivery, `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` metadata, unchanged stored fallback state, no usage line, and one lifecycle fallback event.

Observed result after fix: The patched OpenClaw CLI launched successfully from this checkout and reported version `2026.6.8 (8a8a3a8)`. The current focused regression run completed with `Test Files 1 passed (1)` and `Tests 61 passed (61)`. `git diff --check` produced no output. GitHub `Real behavior proof` passed on current head `39d5ee55683689b7f45083e865be3c68a9e72aa7`.

What was not tested: A real preserved subagent or async fallback run showing the notice reaching the originating visible reply; live Z.AI `ECONNRESET` behavior against a real Z.AI account/provider; and the separate per-provider transport connection/serialization knob requested in #94919.
target.author: monkeyleet
